content.claim: Student assignment to gimnázium programs nationwide is administered through a centralized deferred-acceptance algorithm (used since 2000) that requires schools to rank applicants according to criteria including grades, exams, and interviews (with interview weight capped at 25% by regulation).
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The notes do not provide direct URL to the algorithm specification or to detailed regulatory texts governing weighting criteria.
Algorithmic design does not determine whether early selection should occur; it merely operationalizes whichever entry age is legislated.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
